subject: urgent task list for enron credit inc. - assignment of accounts

emails.subject: Re: Urgent Task List for Enron Credit Inc. - Assignment of Accounts
emails.subject_base: Urgent Task List for Enron Credit Inc. - Assignment of Accounts
emails.body: Teammates, It is obviously a good idea if I am kept in the loop about what is going on with respect to any of the brokerage account projects and assignments or we figure out a way to clarify the recurring confusion on who is working on what or what the status of the projects are. In the meeting yesterday Stephanie, you stated that you had not gotten any feedback on this project yet and therefore had not started any of the tasks. But now you are saying that everything is done and Sara has reviewed it. When did that happen and why did you not send it to me to review? Did I miss something? Have any other of our plans with respect to this project changed? I suggest that at our next meeting we set aside time for everyone to discuss any concerns or quite frankly frustrations any of us have about working on this project along with suggestions on how to better accomplish our goals. Cheryl Nelson Senior Counsel EB3880D (713) 345-4693 Stephanie Panus 02/27/2001 09:36 AM

emails.subject: Re: Urgent Task List for Enron Credit Inc. - Assignment of Accounts
emails.subject_base: Urgent Task List for Enron Credit Inc. - Assignment of Accounts
emails.body: Cheryl, I stated that I had not begun working on the Prime Brokerage Agreements, as you stated in your previous memo that you were going to handle these. I worked on the items that you asked me to work on in your same memo. As you were out most of last week and Sara had received calls on this project, I had Sara review the Authorized Traders List. 02/27/2001 11:20 AM Cheryl Nelson Cheryl Nelson Cheryl Nelson 02/27/2001 11:20 AM 02/27/2001 11:20 AM Sent by: Cheryl Nelson
